mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output, which raises corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when INDPRO has risen over the prior three months, or Rank IC is negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to downward revisions in corporate revenue forecasts and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in both industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signal a coordinated recovery in output and employment that boosts aggregate income and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns follow fewer than 60% of joint INDPRO and PAYEMS increase observations, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over individual series in a logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate policy regime, month-over-month growth in housing starts signals stimulated housing demand and construction activity that spills over into broader economic recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns follow fewer than 55% of HOUST increases when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, or the signal's information coefficient is not statistically different from zero during ZIRP episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales greater than 0.5% signals accelerating consumer spending that directly lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances when RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is below 0.2 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation below 2% reduces the risk of Federal Reserve tightening and supports equity valuations by preserving real discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns follow fewer than 55% of low-inflation observations, or the mean forward return during low-inflation periods is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in industrial production crossing above zero signals a manufacturing momentum inflection that improves capacity utilization and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances when month-over-month INDPRO is positive, or the hit ratio of the signal falls below 50% over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth turning positive signals the end of contraction in aggregate demand and triggers a repricing of cyclical earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns follow fewer than 55% of positive GDP growth readings, or the Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
